name: Condom and Larressingle - loop in the Baïse valley
name_original: Condom et Larressingle – boucle dans la vallée de la Baïse
region_intro: Touring cycling around Condom is characterized by the gentle Gascony countryside, featuring verdant valleys and rolling hills. The region is situated on the Baïse River, which flows through the landscape. This area offers a mix of terrain, from river-adjacent paths to routes traversing agricultural fields. The elevation changes are generally moderate, providing varied cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Condom The most popular touring cycling route is Sunflower Fields of Gers loop from La Sauvetat, an 18.3 miles (29.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This route passes through the agricultural landscapes of the Gers department. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Romieu, Blaziert, and Marsolan Circuit, a moderate 15.4 miles (24.8 km) path. This route connects historical villages and offers views of the Gascony countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Lectoure - Fleurance - Pauilhac Loop, a 20.0 miles (32.2 km) trail leading through the rolling hills of the region, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Touring cycling around Condom is defined by the gentle Gascony countryside, verdant valleys, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes. The routes in Condom are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from nearly 300 reviews. More than 5000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Condom's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Condom?
A: There are over 330 touring cycling routes around Condom, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These include 17 easy routes, 151 moderate routes, and 162 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Condom?
A: Touring cycling around Condom takes you through the gentle Gascony countryside, characterized by verdant valleys and rolling hills. You'll often find yourself cycling alongside the Baïse River, through agricultural fields, and past picturesque villages, offering a scenic and varied experience.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Condom area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Condom are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35629751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sunflower Fields of Gers loop from La Sauvetat&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 29.4 km ride, or the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021117100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Condom and Larressingle - loop in the Baïse valley&lt;/a&gt;, covering 70.1 km.

Q: What historical landmarks can I visit along the cycling routes near Condom?
A: The region is rich in history. You can cycle past sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3770084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larressingle Fortified Village&lt;/a&gt;, often called the 'Carcassonne du Gers', which is a perfectly preserved medieval village. The area is also part of the pilgrimage paths to Santiago de Compostela, featuring historical elements like the Pont d'Artigues. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021110424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Romieu, Blaziert, and Marsolan Circuit&lt;/a&gt; connects several historical villages.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Condom offers 162 difficult touring cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021110751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lectoure, La Romieu et Marsolan – loop in the little French Tuscany&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 48.2 km route with significant elevation changes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021124956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Armagnac Cycling Route&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 78.4 km journey through the region's famous brandy production area.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Condom?
A: The touring cycling routes in Condom are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from nearly 300 reviews. Over 5,000 touring cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic Gascony countryside, the mix of terrain, and the well-maintained paths that connect charming villages.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by lakes or other natural water features?
A: Yes, several routes offer views of or pass near natural water features. The region is situated on the Baïse River, and you can find routes that incorporate river-adjacent paths. Additionally, you can explore areas around lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5812764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Bousquétara&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3201029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Three Valleys&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular spots for relaxation.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes suitable for a half-day trip?
A: Many of the moderate routes are ideal for half-day touring cycling trips. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021112658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lectoure - Fleurance - Pauilhac Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 32.2 km route that typically takes under two hours to complete, making it perfect for a morning or afternoon ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021111173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lectoure and Plieux – loop in the little French Tuscany&lt;/a&gt; is another moderate option at 33.3 km.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Condom?
A: The gentle Gascony countryside is particularly pleasant for touring cycling during spring and early autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures and beautiful scenery, with blooming wildflowers in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable.

Q: Are there routes that showcase the famous sunflower fields of Gers?
A: Yes, the region is known for its stunning sunflower fields. A popular route that highlights these agricultural landscapes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10610933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sunflower Fields of Gers loop from Saint-Puy&lt;/a&gt;, a 37.6 km difficult route that offers picturesque views, especially when the sunflowers are in bloom.

Q: Can I combine cycling with exploring local villages and towns?
A: Absolutely. The touring cycling routes around Condom often connect charming villages and towns. You can plan your rides to include stops in places like La Romieu, known for its collegiate church, or explore the fortified village of Larressingle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021108660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mézinais – Ténarèze Circuit&lt;/a&gt; is a longer route that passes through several such locales.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of castles or châteaux?
A: Yes, the region boasts several historical castles and châteaux. While cycling, you might pass by or near sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5859953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Madirac&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5681633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Terraube&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021117100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Condom and Larressingle - loop in the Baïse valley&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights the fortified village of Larressingle.

Q: What is the general elevation profile for touring cycling in Condom?
A: The elevation changes around Condom are generally moderate, reflecting the rolling hills of the Gascony countryside. While there are challenging routes with significant climbs, many routes feature gentle ascents and descents, providing a varied but manageable cycling experience for most touring cyclists.
tips:
If you are traveling on the GR65, you must make the small detour of about 2 km to visit Larressingle. The Gascons call this little place "Carcassonne du Gers". It is a fine example of the region's fortified villages. The place consists of several houses, the castle and the castle church. It is completely surrounded by an intact fortification wall and a moat.
Construction of Larrissingle began in 1286. It was there that the Abbots of Lectoure found a safe place. In the 16th century, however, the abbots got a new country seat. Larrissingle was meaningless after that and time still seems to have stood still. A beautiful, authentic example of the Middle Ages.

Small town in the Pays d'Auch, known for its Feria de la Pentecôte, the Pentecotavic, during which a superb festival of brass bands takes place. To see: the Chapter Tower, the Cordeliers convent, the arenas and the few half-timbered houses that remain in the town centre.

This town of barely 1,150 inhabitants has a rich architectural heritage including the Saint-Jean-Baptiste church and its superb square as well as the Cistercian abbey of Flaran, daughter of the Escaladieu abbey.

Condom is a Gascon episcopal city, stronghold of the most famous of musketeers: d'Artagnan. The statue of the 4 musketeers by Dumas has stood in Saint Peter's Square since its inauguration in 2010. It is the work of Georgian sculptor Zurab Tsereteli. This town is crossed by the cycle route 82 known as the Baïse valley. The old center is remarkable. The city is also very lively and offers many activities to those who are just passing through, such as introductory workshops in animated cinema.

This cathedral, former diocese of Condom between 1317 and 1790, has been classified as a historic monument since 1840. We can easily see the scars of the wars of religion on its facades as well as in the heart of its magnificent cloister with Gothic vaults. Since 2010, the statue of the Dumas musketeers has stood on the cathedral square. It was created by the Georgian sculptor Zurab Tseretel.

waypoints.name: Statue of the Three Musketeers at Saint-Pierre Cathedral, Condom
